job_description:
Job Title

QC Lead

Department

Quality Assurance / Quality Control

Location

Kochi, Kerala

Reporting To

Technical Manager / Engineering Manager / Delivery Manager

Employment Type

Full-Time

Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced and detail‑oriented QC Lead to lead our Quality Control team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for planning, managing, and executing software testing activities while ensuring the delivery of high‑quality software products. The QC Lead will mentor QA engineers, establish testing standards, improve testing processes, and collaborate closely with Development, Product, and Project Management teams to ensure product quality throughout the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC).

Key Responsibilities

Team Leadership

  • Lead and manage the Quality Control team.
  • Assign testing activities based on project priorities.
  • Mentor and guide QA Engineers and Senior QA Engineers.
  • Conduct performance reviews and provide constructive feedback.
  • Identify training needs and support team development.

Test Planning & Strategy

  • Prepare Test Strategy and Test Plans for projects.
  • Estimate testing efforts and resource requirements.
  • Define testing scope, timelines, and deliverables.
  • Review testing schedules with project stakeholders.

Quality Assurance Activities

  • Review Business Requirements Documents (BRD), Functional Requirement Specifications (FRS), and User Stories.
  • Ensure complete test coverage.
  • Create and review Test Cases, Test Scenarios, and Test Data.
  • Validate software against business requirements.
  • Perform Functional, Integration, Regression, System, Smoke, and User Acceptance Testing support.

Defect Management

  • Track and manage defects using defect tracking tools.
  • Conduct defect triage meetings.
  • Work with developers to resolve issues.
  • Verify fixes before release.
  • Monitor defect leakage and root cause analysis.

Automation

  • Identify opportunities for test automation.
  • Review automation scripts developed by QA Engineers.
  • Work with Automation Engineers to improve regression coverage.
  • Promote automation best practices.

Process Improvement

  • Improve QA processes and methodologies.
  • Develop QA standards and SOPs.
  • Monitor QA KPIs and quality metrics.
  • Ensure compliance with ISO, CMMI, or internal quality standards.
  • Participate in internal quality audits.

Release Management Support

  • Participate in Sprint Planning.
  • Attend Daily Scrum meetings.
  • Support Go‑Live activities.
  • Perform Release Validation Testing.
  • Ensure production readiness.

Documentation

  • Prepare Test Reports, Weekly QA Status Reports, Release Readiness Reports, Quality Metrics Dashboard, Defect Summary Reports, Test Closure Reports.

Stakeholder Communication

  • Coordinate with Product Owners, Business Analysts, Developers, and Project Managers.
  • Present QA status during project meetings.
  • Escalate quality risks proactively.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related field.
  • 6–10 years of experience in Software Testing / Quality Control.
  • Minimum 2 years of experience leading a QA/QC team.
  • Strong understanding of SDLC and STLC.
  • Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments.

Required Technical Skills

  • Manual Testing
  • Test Planning & Test Strategy
  • Test Case Design
  • Defect Management
  • Regression, Integration, API, Database (SQL) Testing
  • Automation Testing concepts
  • Jira, Azure DevOps
  • TestRail / Zephyr
  • Postman
  • Git (basic understanding)

Preferred Skills

  • Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, Appium
  • JMeter, REST API Testing, Performance Testing concepts
  • CI/CD Testing, Docker (basic understanding)
  • AI‑assisted Testing tools

Soft Skills

  • Strong leadership
  • Excellent communication
  • Problem‑solving ability
  • Analytical thinking
  • Decision‑making
  • Time management
  • Team mentoring
  • Stakeholder management
  • Attention to detail
  • Conflict resolution

KPIs (Key Performance Indicators)

  • Test Case Coverage ≥ 95%
  • Defect Leakage ≤ 3%
  • Test Execution on Schedule ≥ 95%
  • Regression Coverage ≥ 100%
  • UAT Defect Escape Rate
  • Release Quality Score
  • Automation Coverage Growth
  • Team Productivity
  • On‑Time Delivery
  • Customer Satisfaction

Notice Period

Immediate to 15 Days

Preferred Location

Kochi, Kerala

Application

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at thradmin@distinctinfotech.com.
summary: QC Lead oversees QA team, plans and executes testing, ensures high‑quality software delivery. Requires 6‑10 years experience and strong leadership.

Closing Date: 31 Aug 2026 Job Published: 22 Jul 2026 Contact Email: Vialli.marques@worldwide.com

Summary

The Business Analyst acts as a consultative business partner responsible for bridging the gap between business challenges and data‑driven solutions. This role focuses on deeply understanding business problems to gather and refine requirements for business intelligence, interactive dashboards, and advanced visualizations using Power BI and the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform (including SQL and AI/BI).

  • Partner with business owners to identify core business problems and translate them into actionable requirements for business intelligence and data products.
  • Facilitate discovery sessions to gather requirements for dashboards and visualizations, ensuring alignment between stakeholder expectations and technical feasibility.
  • Decompose high‑level business needs into detailed functional requirements for implementation teams specializing in Databricks SQL and Power BI development.
  • Utilize Databricks AI/BI capabilities to enable self‑service analytics and natural language data exploration for non‑technical stakeholders.
  • Manage the delivery lifecycle for BI products, whether developed internally or by external partners, ensuring compliance with data and visualization standards.
  • Conduct User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for all dashboards and reports to ensure they provide the intended business value and answer the primary business questions.
  • Serve as the primary liaison between business units and data engineering teams to ensure underlying data models support complex reporting requirements.
  • Perform other duties as assigned.

Preferred Skills & Abilities

  • Advanced elicitation and questioning techniques to uncover the root business need rather than just "feature requests."
  • Stakeholder analysis to manage influence and authority levels across various clinical and corporate departments.
  • Expertise in process and data modeling to ensure BI solutions are built on a foundation of clean, logical business rules.
  • Facilitation skills for leading requirements workshops and steering committees focused on data strategy.
  • Understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) in an Agile or iterative environment, specifically as it applies to BI and data product delivery.
  • Interface analysis to understand how data flows from source systems into the Databricks Lakehouse for reporting.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field.
  • 2+ years of proven experience analyzing business requirements and documenting them for successful BI or data intelligence projects.
  • Direct experience with Power BI.
  • Experience in the conduct of clinical trials and an understanding of the data generated during the clinical lifecycle OR significant experience in business analysis for large‑scale enterprise data systems.
  • Strong analytical thinking with a focus on providing "intelligence" rather than just "reporting."
summary: Business Analyst drives data‑driven insights using Power BI and Databricks, translating business problems into actionable BI solutions. Requires 2+ years experience.

We are looking for a skilled Data Engineer to design, build, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines and API‑centric integrations for our Databricks Lakehouse supporting clinical trial data.

Key Responsibilities

Data Pipeline & Lakehouse Engineering

  • Build and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines ingesting data from clinical trial management systems and third‑party vendors.
  • Manage and optimize the Databricks Lakehouse architecture, implementing the Medallion Architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold).
  • Perform expert‑level SQL development and performance tuning on SQL Server 2016.
  • Transition legacy batch jobs to modern event‑driven or request‑response API patterns.

API‑Centric Integration Development

  • Design and deploy robust APIs enabling Databricks Lakehouse to consume data directly from Medidata, Salesforce, Workday, Certinia, NetSuite.
  • Build API endpoints for real‑time clinical insights and financial metrics.
  • Implement Databricks Unity Catalog as a governance layer for secure cross‑system API connections.

Customer Data Access & Sharing

  • Develop and maintain the API suite for customers to access their clinical data.
  • Implement Databricks Delta Sharing for secure, direct access to live tables without traditional ETL.
  • Create comprehensive API documentation and developer portals.

Data Modeling & Analytics Enablement

  • Develop and maintain data models within the lakehouse to support advanced analytics and clinical reporting.
  • Collaborate with analysts, AI engineers, and data scientists to curate datasets.

Data Governance, Quality & Security

  • Design and implement data quality and monitoring solutions.
  • Use Microsoft Purview to map data lineage across API boundaries.
  • Ensure API integrations meet security standards (OAuth2, OpenID Connect) and compliance (GxP, HIPAA).
  • Monitor API performance and latency; maintain documentation for lineage, schemas, and pipeline logic.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field.
  • 3-5 years professional experience in data engineering, warehousing, or systems integration.
  • Strong proficiency in SQL, specifically SQL Server 2016.
  • Hands‑on experience with Databricks (Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Delta Sharing).
  • Experience building ETL pipelines using SSIS, Azure Data Factory, dbt, or Lakeflow.
  • Expert‑level experience building and consuming RESTful APIs using Python, C#, or Scala.
  • Hands‑on experience with API ecosystems of Salesforce, Workday, NetSuite.
  • Solid understanding of data modeling (dimensional and relational).
  • Experience with cloud storage such as Azure Data Lake.
  • Knowledge of zero‑copy architecture and data virtualization.
  • Familiarity with Microsoft Purview and/or Unity Catalog for metadata management.

Preferred Skills

  • Experience in Life Sciences or Clinical Research (CRO) industry.
  • Familiarity with clinical data standards (CDISC, Medidata).
  • Proficiency in Python for data processing and automation.
  • Experience with Git and CI/CD deployment workflows.

All responsibilities and qualifications are retained from the original posting.
summary: Data Engineer needed to build ETL pipelines and APIs for a Databricks Lakehouse in clinical research. Requires 3-5 years with SQL Server and Azure.
